agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher population in a ZIP code naturally results in a higher count of social service organizations to meet the demands of a larger resident population.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Bielefeld (2000) and Wolch (1990) show that public service and nonprofit density correlates strongly with local demographic populations.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A highly statistically significant positive correlation was was found. ZIP codes with larger populations contain a higher raw count of social service organizations, reflecting local market demand scaling.
